customTitle: Why does my zsh start so slow?

message.content: My zsh takes ~2 seconds to open a new shell. Where do I even start looking?

content.text: Profile it. Add `zmodload zsh/zprof` as the very first line of `.zshrc` and `zprof` as the very last line. Open a new shell — you'll get a sorted table of where time was spent. Common culprits in order of frequency: (1) NVM's `nvm.sh` (lazy-load it); (2) compinit running without `-C` (cache the completions: `compinit -C`); (3) p10k / starship doing synchronous git status in a deep repo (use the async variants); (4) `conda init` shell hook running on every shell instead of activated on demand. Fix one at a time and recheck — usually one offender accounts for >60% of the time.
